summary: The session focused on significant historical events that occurred in 1971, highlighting their impact on global politics and economics. The user inquired about the deviations in charts post-1971, prompting a discussion on various key events from that year.
facts: The United States ended the convertibility of the US dollar to gold in 1971, ending the Bretton Woods system of fixed exchange rates. The Bangladesh Liberation War took place in 1971, resulting in the independence of Bangladesh from Pakistan. The United Kingdom and the Irish Republic signed the Anglo-Irish Treaty in 1971, establishing the Irish Free State and ending British rule in most of Ireland. The U.S. Supreme Court upheld the use of busing to integrate schools in 1971 in the landmark case Swann v. Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education. The United States and the Soviet Union signed the Strategic Arms Limitation Talks (SALT I) treaty in 1971, establishing limits on the number of nuclear weapons each side could possess. The United Nations General Assembly declared in 1971 that "zip codes" should be used worldwide. The first microprocessor, the Intel 4004, was released in 1971.
